artPrompt: A shuttered city hall at the edge of a feral town square, its doors chained and weeds climbing the steps, lit by the ember-orange glow of distant bonfires, no figures present. Overturned ballot boxes and torn blank bunting litter the foreground, smoke bleeding into night-black up the wide frame. A long-abandoned civic seat amid mayhem, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Precinct of Perpetual Recess
text:
Precinct of Perpetual Recess enters the battlefield tapped, then untaps the moment somebody at the table makes a joke. (House rules: groaners count.)
{T}: Add {R}.
{T}: Add {R}{R}, but only until your next snack. After your next snack, the energy is gone and so is your appetite for governance.
flavorText: City hall is closed. It has been closed for eleven years. Nobody minds.
